description_long: This is a short section of a wooden picket fence, featuring five vertical slats with pointed tops. The slats are evenly spaced and attached to two horizontal rails, one of which is visible as slightly angled, giving the fence a rustic and handmade appearance. The wood has a light, weathered finish with visible grain and some subtle discoloration, suggesting outdoor exposure. The pointed tops of the pickets are cut into a triangular shape, providing a classic suburban or garden fence look. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, and the construction appears simple and functional. This type of fence is commonly used to demarcate property boundaries or enclose gardens in urban and suburban environments.
description: A light, weathered wooden picket fence section with five pointed slats and two horizontal rails, showing a rustic, handmade appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the fence and the pointed tops of the pickets.
description_view_1: Front view displaying five evenly spaced, pointed pickets and the angled horizontal rail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and pointed tops of the pickets.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the pickets and the more prominent horizontal rail attachment.
